TicketDescription: The application is displaying an "The specified resource was not found" error when trying to access a static asset (e.g. image).
SuggestedSolution:
Add app.UseStaticFiles(); in your Program.cs file before routing/endpoint mapping.
Incorrect File Location: Static files must be placed within the wwwroot folder by default.
Incorrect URL Path: If your file is at wwwroot/images/photo.jpg, the URL should be https://localhost:port/images/photo.jpg. Do not include wwwroot in the URL.
Case Sensitivity: On Linux hosting environments, file paths are case-sensitive. Image.png is not the same as image.png [19].
Unknown File Types: If the file extension is not recognized (e.g..apk), the static file middleware will return a 404.

TicketDescription: Invalid date format in the query error message: 'Invalid date format'.
SuggestedSolution:
First, determine the correct date format required by the database, API, or system you are querying. 
Standard SQL (ISO 8601): The widely accepted and recommended format is YYYY-MM-DD (e.g., 2025-04-15).
Database-Specific Defaults: Different systems have different defaults:
MySQL/PostgreSQL/SQL Server (DATE type): YYYY-MM-DD.
US English Locale: Often MM/DD/YYYY (e.g., 04/15/2025).
European Locale: Often DD/MM/YYYY (e.g., 15/04/2025)
